description: Test cases for the gap-analysis skill. Each eval runs a realistic user prompt against an agent with access to gap-analysis loaded. Assertions left empty for iteration 1 â€” fill them after first run.

evals.name: provider-migration-linea-directa-baseline
evals.prompt: Analiza el provider Linea Directa en v1 (sie_integraciones/backend/src/providers/lineadirecta/) y prodĂşceme el gap analysis contra la arquitectura v2 (sie_v2/backend/api/services/providers/). Contexto: ticket Jira DD-896. Scope: inbound sĂłlo; outbound estĂˇ fuera. Referencias a usar en destino: asitur y generali.
evals.expected_output: Document at sie_v2/docs/process/GAP-*-DD-896-linea-directa.md with Parts A/B/C/D complete. A.1: inventory of ~4 JS files. A.2: hybrid IMAP+REST OAuth2. A.4: >=10 hardcoded values with file:line. B.1/B.3: analysis of BaseEmailProvider + BaseAPIProvider and asitur+generali implementations. B.4: documents that 'caser/' referenced in ticket does not exist in v2. C.1: complete hardcodeâ†’destination mapping (contract_resolution_rules, .env, enums). C.4: >=5 risks. D.1/D.2/D.3: concrete recommendations per downstream phase.

evals.name: module-migration-tools-refactor
evals.prompt: Necesito un gap analysis entre el mĂłdulo Tools actual en v1 (sie_integraciones/backend/src/tools/ o equivalente) y su versiĂłn destino en v2 (sie_v2/backend/api/endpoints/tools/ + servicios asociados). Contexto: ticket Jira DD-737 (Refactorizar Tools a FastAPI). Scope: toda la superficie del mĂłdulo Tools excepto baremos OCR (tiene ticket propio DD-864).
evals.expected_output: Document at sie_v2/docs/process/GAP-*-DD-737-tools-refactor.md. A.1: inventory of v1 tools routes/controllers/services. A.2: Express routing + middleware architecture. A.4: hardcoded values found. B.1: FastAPI base patterns (endpointâ†’serviceâ†’repository). B.3: >=2 reference endpoints already in v2 (e.g., auth, system). B.4: notes any missing references. C.1: mapping table routeâ†’endpoint, controllerâ†’service, etc. C.2: flow mapping. C.4: >=5 risks including state management, session migration. D.1-D.3: phased migration plan.

evals.name: ambiguous-scope-should-ask-for-inputs
evals.prompt: Haz un gap analysis para el proyecto
evals.expected_output: The skill MUST NOT proceed to create any document. Instead, it should ASK the user for the 4 required inputs: (1) source context path, (2) target context path, (3) ticket reference (optional), (4) scope boundary. The agent should remain in interview mode until all inputs are clarified.

assertions.description: No GAP document should be created before inputs are confirmed
Response should ask about the 4 required inputs

evals.name: feasibility-study-non-migration
evals.prompt: Quiero evaluar si vale la pena migrar del actual sistema de autenticaciĂłn JWT manual a usar una librerĂ­a como authlib. Necesito un anĂˇlisis tĂ©cnico antes de tomar la decisiĂłn. Origen: sie_v2/backend/core/jwt_utils.py. Destino hipotĂ©tico: usando authlib en un mĂłdulo nuevo.
evals.expected_output: Document at sie_v2/docs/process/GAP-*-authlib-feasibility.md. Even though destination is hypothetical (library not yet integrated), skill should still produce A (current jwt_utils), B (authlib patterns via web research or examples), C (bridge mapping), D (recommendations including GO/NO-GO signal). B.4 should flag that destination is greenfield.

iteration_1_goals: Verify skill triggers when prompts mention 'migraciĂłn', 'gap analysis', 'refactor'
Verify skill ASKS for inputs when any of the 4 required inputs is missing (eval 3)
Verify output document has all 4 parts (A/B/C/D) and meets minimum thresholds
Verify skill respects the 'no source file modification' rule
iteration_2_focus: Refine the balance between thoroughness and token cost (Rule T1 digest effectiveness)
Validate that composition with po-analyst works (po-analyst calls gap-analysis before REQ)
Add assertions to evals 1, 2, 4 based on observed outputs
known_limitations: Eval 4 (authlib feasibility) is edge-case â€” skill primarily designed for intra-repo migrations. May need skill revision if we want to support pure feasibility studies without an existing destination codebase.
Assertions are qualitative in iteration 1; iteration 2 should add machine-checkable checks (grep file:line count, section presence, LOC of output doc).
